scheme_name: Post Matric Scholarship for SC Students
ministry_department: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
short_description: Financial assistance for SC students pursuing post-matriculation or post-secondary education.
detailed_description: The Post Matric Scholarship Scheme for Scheduled Caste students is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme to provide financial assistance to SC students studying at post-matriculation or post-secondary stage to enable them to complete their education. The scheme covers maintenance allowance, reimbursement of compulsory non-refundable fees, study tour charges, thesis typing/printing charges for research scholars, and book allowance.
key_benefits: Maintenance allowance ranging from ₹380 to ₹1,200 per month depending on course
Book allowance and study tour charges
Thesis typing charges for research scholars
income_criteria: Parental income not exceeding ₹2.5 Lakhs per annum
other_conditions: Must belong to Scheduled Caste category
Must have passed matriculation or higher secondary examination
Must be pursuing recognized post-matriculation course
Only two children of the same parents/guardian are eligible

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Valid SC caste certificate issued by competent authority
Parental income certificate not exceeding ₹2.5 Lakhs per annum
Current year fee receipt from institution
Student's bank account for scholarship transfer

application_steps_online: Visit National Scholarship Portal at scholarships.gov.in
Register as new user with valid email and mobile number
Login and select 'Post Matric Scholarship for SC Students'
Fill application form with personal, academic and income details
Upload all required documents in specified format
Submit application and save application ID
application_steps_offline: Obtain application form for SC Post Matric Scholarship
Fill form completely with accurate information
Attach self-attested copies of all required documents
Institution will verify and forward to state authorities

submission_locations.notes: Institution will verify documents and forward application

faq.question: Can I apply if I'm studying in a private institution?
faq.answer: Yes, students in both government and private recognized institutions are eligible.

faq.question: What is the income limit?
faq.answer: Parental income should not exceed ₹2.5 Lakhs per annum from all sources.

common_rejection_reasons: More than two siblings already receiving scholarship
tips_for_successful_application: Ensure caste certificate is issued by competent authority
Get income certificate from Tehsildar or equivalent officer
Apply before deadline to avoid last-minute issues
